name: Alma Bassett
motto: “She sports a witching gown, With a ruffle up and down. She is gentle, she is shy; Yet there’s wisdom in her eye, For she can look as wise As grave Minerva’s Owl.”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21

name: Cora Beggs
clubs: Sponsor of Class of '21 of U. of F. 1921
description: The why we have for loving Dot Is not because of size she's got, It's all the things that she can do, It's just her pep that puts them thru.

name: Eleanor Brewer
description: Hearty and joyous and true, Ready to help on a laugh; Believes in a good carry thru In ev'ything from golf club to bat— Brewer, we hand it to you: With baseball and discus you've got 'em. You outswim the rest of the crew— And quail on a string? You shot 'em!

name: Marie Bryan
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
President of Florida Collegiate Press Association 1920-'21
description: She's a peach without a doubt— When you know her you'll find that out. 'SMarie runs the old Flambeau, And she sure has made it go. Soon when the "Gordon" knot is tied She will be a blushing bride. They say he loves her too, And indeed I know I do.

name: Grace Burwell
motto: “She’s never cross, She’s never mean; A sweeter girl We’ve never seen.”

name: Kate Byrd
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19
Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
Vice-President of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: "We find our little Katie Byrd (Who's very versatile I've heard) In baseball 'n' Y. W. C. A. As well as in the orchestra."

name: Maude Clyatt, B.S.
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
description: A mop of curly hair, A little figure high in the air; A scream, "Who threw that goal?" "It's Maude, you silly soul!" It's just the same, For with one aim The task is always complete. Three cheers for Maude, "Our Athlete."

name: Elizabeth Conradi
clubs: Sponsor of Class of '21 of U. of F. 1921
description: Praise of her eloquence might fill a book, Of her charm, of her poise and look; And more might be said, but this is so, A great, great reader some day we'll know.

name: Genevieve Duggan
clubs: Class Representative on Executive Committee of Student Government, 1918-'19
description: When the world seems to frown on you, And you just can't keep from feeling blue, Think of the smile of Genevieve, And all your troubles will surely leave.

name: Allie Lou Felton
motto: "She can think, and she thinks clearly, Plays the game, and speaks sincerely; And as she goes we find to praise Work done squarely and unwasted days."
clubs: Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1916-'17
Representative at Large on Athletic Board 1917-'18
Class Representative on Executive Committee of Student Government 1917-'18

name: Reva Fletcher
motto: "Nor bold nor shy, Nor short nor tall, But a nice mingling Of them all."
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21

name: Marguerite Folsom
motto: “Seniors great and Seniors small, Seniors short and Seniors tall, And among them all I see One who seems the best to me! Finest eyes of darkest brown, Sweetest smile for those around, Cheerful words for those who’re blue— That’s what she does for me and you!”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19-'20
Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21

name: Rosalia Gonzalez
motto: "For to tell who and why you are or ain't, No poet I, but this my plaint: 'To those who know thee not No words can paint. And those who know thee, Know all words are faint.'"
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
Sponsor of Class of '21 of U. of F. 1921

name: May Gradick
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19-'20
Undergraduate Representative of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: “‘What makes Blue Ridge blue, girls?’ May is heard to sing, While on her way to classes— In fact in everything. ‘While the organ peeled bananas’— Oh! murder! I’ll go insane! If she only would hush that singing So a fellow could use his brain.”

name: Gladys Greene
motto: "Here's to Gladys, our dear friend, Who scatters joy where'er she trends; Sweet gentle nature, so loving and true. Her friends are many, her enemies few."

name: Mildred Hall
clubs: Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
Sponsor of Class of '21 of U. of F. 1920, 1921
description: Coy, cold, fickle, and shy, Skinny, short, innocent, O, my! In fact her loveliness I never knew Until her smile flashed in view. Then I saw her life was bright, A well of joy, a spring of light. O! she's fair, as many Seniors be, But Milly's the pick. Look about and see.

name: PRICILLA HAMM
description: Priscilla wed John Alden in the years long past away; And may I wish the same thing for Priscilla of today? But for our modern Pris I think we'd do a better job If just for her convenience we should change John's name to Bob.

name: Grace Earle Hildreth
motto: “Thou butterfly from Gay Paree, I wish the best of luck to thee, But where most butterflies like light I know you want eternal ‘Knight’. And when it comes your time to pick, I wish much joy to you and Dick..”
clubs: Vice-President of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
Sponsor of Class of '21 of U. of F. 1920
President of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21

name: Ruth Holmer
motto: “She’s got a heart that is big as the world, And a smile that beats that of any girl. What more could she have!!”

name: Mary Irene Johnson
motto: “As true a friend as one could wish You’ll find this girl, Irene. Along such lines as cookery Her knowledge is supreme.”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21

name: Johnson, Leila Love
description: As fresh as the dawn of a summer's day; She studies never a bit, they say. But I look at all her marks And wearily wonder at her larks— My mind is numb, In fact it's dumb, Why couldn't we all be born that way?

name: Lewella Jones
motto: "I never cared for teacher's pet— Even one of Mr. Pi's. I wouldn't be one on a bet; But when into Lewella's eyes I look, why, I forget that part, Because Lewella's vamped my heart."
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19

name: Olga Kent
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20-'21
Assistant in Home Economics Research Laboratory 1921
description: Young—yes, and clever, That's Miss Olga Kent. When she's not at science O'er English she's bent. In sewing and cooking She sure does excel, And also in research Does equally well. She looks after mousies, Some large and some small; Some robust and healthy, And some not at all.

name: Clara Kibler
motto: "Her portrait fair upon my mind, Always there, ne'er hard to find. With eyes that dance, and tongue that goes, Clara's a girl whom everyone knows."

name: Augusta Laxton
motto: "There is a young lady named Gus; They say she can make quite a fuss. She's exceedingly bright right in the head; As for her hair—I wouldn't say it was red. She is 'bout the nicest girl I know, But please don't tell her I told you so."
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21

name: Julia Linebaugh
clubs: Secretary of Executive Committee of Student Government 1918-'19
Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: "Look about and you will see The finest girl that e'er did be In Y. W., athletics, or on 'S. G.' In fact there's nothing a fellow can say That Julia hasn't done in her day, And always it was in the grandest way."

name: Willie Lipscombe
clubs: Class Representative on Executive Committee of Student Government 1919-'20
description: Dear Bill, you're good in History, And good in Math also. In Poly. Ec. 'n' Chemistry, You've got a mighty good show. I like all this about you, Bill, but What I like the best Is this: you're as good in friendship as You are in all the rest.

name: Hallie Little
motto: “Be gone, dull care, I pray thee be gone from me. Be gone, dull care, thou and I shall never agree.”

name: Katherine Martin
description: Please accept from your Soph Sister The best love she can give; And know that in the future In her memory you will live. And no matter what the number Of my years should chance to be, I'll ne'er forget our happy days At F. S. W. C.

name: Winifred Mason
clubs: Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19, 1920-'21
Secretary of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
description: "Oh, tell me where I'll find her, you know the girl I mean. Her hair is kinky, inky black, and she poses like a queen." I thought a minute doubtfully, and cast my eyes toward heaven, And then I answered, "Why, she's always in three hundred twenty-seven."

name: Margaret Miller
description: Jolly 'n' funny 'n' full o' pep, We could always count on her— 'Cause that was her rep.

name: Ernestine Mitchell
clubs: Sponsor of Class of '21 of U. of F. 1920
Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920
Delegate to National Y. W. C. A. Convention 1920
Secretary of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: Ernestine, you made the annual, and we're much obliged to you. We know whene'er we ask you that your very best you'll do. In track, or on the tennis court, We know you're just as good a sport, With your dancing eyes and your soft brown curls You're just one of those 'all-round' girls.

name: Alice Mosier
motto: "This year you say good bye to school And leave old F. S. C. And I can never tell you How much you've meant to me. And may I ask one favor? When your cap and gown you doff In all the years that are to come Don't forget your Sister Soph."

name: Murphey, Willella
motto: “Always laughing, never sad, Full of fun and often bad, Quite intellectual, but would you know? She’s most twenty and has a beau.”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19

name: Malena Murray
description: Mathematics is a bore, So 'tis often said; But Malena thinks it's nothing more than play, Plus a little 'head'. Trig, Calculus and Analyt, She knows them one and all, But her charm and winning way Is why for her we fall.

name: McDavid, Myrtie
description: Myrt's come rolling thru the years With college trails and school girl fears. She's made her mark, attained her goal, Her smile's an example for every soul.

name: Mary Odom
description: Mary is her name, And for good lessons she is always famed. But when you grow to know her well, So her disposition you can tell, And see the jokes she pulls off by her wit so keen, A jollier person you will find you've never seen.

name: Ora Odom
description: “Here’s to our domestic queen, Who to sit on a cushion And sew a fine seam May always be hers, But not alone, For she is full worthy Of a happy home.”

name: Clara Opsahl
description: Her winning ways and lots of pep You know we all adore. Her studiousness has won her rep, Which makes us love her more.

name: Faith Potter
motto: "For rare intelligence And brightness of mind, Indifference and calmness of mien, Tempered with kindness and friendship to all, Her equal has never been seen."
clubs: Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
Class Representative on Executive Committee of Student Government 1920-'21

name: Lillian Powell
motto: "You've been loyal, honest and brave, And have worked with a very good will, And though you have labored and striven You have profited by every hard drill."

name: Eva Richardson
description: "To my Senior Sister, in these lines I would a tribute pay; And tho not gifted in making rhymes, This I want to say: 'If the best of the North and of the South And of the East and West Were brought together—indeed you'd be the best.'"

name: Luella Rouse
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: Now who doesn't know Luella? Why, she's Y. W.'s best tag seller. She always works for this and other causes, And without her goal she never pauses. I'll tell you, my Senior Sisters, she's a shark, And another good thing, she likes a lark.

name: Mildred Schultz
clubs: Chairman of Board of Managers of Flambeau 1920-'21
description: Mildred Schultz is her name, Thru History and Science she'll ne'er win her fame. But in the arts She'll play her part And 'Come right thru smilin'.'

name: Velma Shands
motto: “You are witty, you are clever, You’re an actress, we can tell. We’ll forget you? Ah, no, never, ‘Sweets to the sweet Farewell.’”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1916-'17-'18-'19

name: Alice Shearston
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: She doesn't say much, 'tis true, But just let me tell you She has the brains right there And a poetic gift so rare— She is as sweet as she can be— When you know her, you will see.

name: Anne Shockley
description: When you asked me to be your Soph Sister I was thrilled to a million or more To think that I'd be your companion As you left the college door. And, Annie, so long as I've known you, I've got to hand it to you That tho you are 'all calm without' You're always pepped clear thru.

name: Janie Smith
motto: "Brown eyes, black hair, The whole world knows She's sweet and fair. With a nature so gentle And a heart so true, A 'love nest' for two, dear, Is the future for you."

name: Smith, Florence
clubs: Treasurer of Executive Committee of Student Government 1918-'19
Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19-'20
description: "A very fine girl is Florence Smith, And a very fine girl is she. Tho she calls people up, And she calls people down, But she is on 'S. G.,' you see."

name: Loyola Stacy
motto: “So sweet and so gay, The same on a sunny or rainy day. If you are in trouble To her quickly run, And she'll end said trouble 'Fore it's scarcely begun.”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1918-'19
Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20-'21

name: Marion Stine
motto: “Small and comely, eyes sparkly and blue, When Marion’s a friend, she’s a friend who’s true. That a Chemistry Major is deep we admit, But it phases Maid Marion—no, not a whit. She hails from Virginia; hospitable, free, That she reflects this warm spirit all will agree.”

name: Eileen Vivian
description: She shows the world a gracious air, For she is tall and slim and fair, Sweet melodies she often plays, And e'en the jazz of modern days. A 'good sport through and through' is she Who kindness gives unstintingly.

name: Floy Wharton
motto: “Here's to you, my Senior Sister, Of all I know the best, And for you I wouldn't take a million Of those like the rest. You may be small, but you know It's quality we find That makes the world, where'er we go So big and true and kind.”
clubs: Larger Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1919-'20
Class Representative on Executive Committee of Student Government 1919-'20

name: Elizabeth Williams
clubs: Vice-President of Executive Committee of Student Government 1919-'20
President of Executive Committee of Student Government 1920-'21
Smaller Cabinet of Y. W. C. A. 1920-'21
description: Of all Senior Sisters in this world 'Slim' is by far the best. Look where you may, you'll ne'er find a girl So different from the rest. She's pretty and sweet, She's tall and neat, And taken all together Just can't be beat.

name: Orabel Wynn
motto: "She's always sunny and happy, With a loving word or two; Looking on the bright side Rather than the blue."

name: Sue Yent
motto: "She lives in the world of classics— In Latin lies her fame; Jolly, gay and full of fun, She always is the same. President of Classical Club, Sue Yent is her name."
